worldview: On the fixed pr1394 SP8192/GPTQ/recurrence/MuonEq-R/SDClip base, credible upside is more likely to come from searching explicit transition and selection programs aligned to downstream sliding-window bpb than from mutating local scalars inside an already-strong stack.

hypotheses.name: Bootstrap-to-Receiver Handoff Search
hypotheses.mechanism: Keep the published pr1394 stack as a shared bootstrap trunk, then switch once into one of several late receiver programs that spend the remaining budget differently for downstream-friendly finishing; the searchable object is the handoff policy, not a patch list.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: A single fixed training program should govern all steps on this base.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This changes the unit of search from scalar schedule settings to a two-stage executable program with a frozen shared trunk and an explicit late-policy identity.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Run 2 no-handoff controls plus up to 6 receiver candidates that all share the same bootstrap segment and differ only in receiver identity and handoff policy; require at least 2 positive realizations on downstream sliding-window bpb to survive.
hypotheses.expected_observable: If real, separation should appear mainly in downstream sliding-window bpb after the handoff region, with pre-quant bpb moving less than the deployed metric.
hypotheses.falsifier: All receiver variants remain inside control spread, or any apparent gain is pre-quant only while downstream sliding-window bpb regresses.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: This operationalizes the hm2-style transition-program idea on the real SP8192/pr1394 base instead of reverting to static patch packs.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Passes as a consequential regime-transition family with a crisp control structure; executable claim is plausible but still tied to the existing handoff catalog rather than new machinery.

hypotheses.name: Same-Checkpoint Export Selector Family
hypotheses.mechanism: Hold training fixed and search a selector over checkpoint or export states from the same run window, choosing deployable artifacts by downstream sliding-window bpb instead of defaulting to one final exported state.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: The last trained state is automatically the best deployable artifact.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This changes which artifact definition competes, not a small quantization scalar inside one fixed artifact path.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Use 2 controls with the published final-state export rule and up to 6 selector candidates that compare a small checkpoint/export portfolio from otherwise identical training traces.
hypotheses.expected_observable: Downstream sliding-window bpb should move while pre-quant bpb remains almost unchanged, showing that the gain came from artifact choice rather than training dynamics.
hypotheses.falsifier: Selector candidates show no downstream separation beyond control spread, or only trade bpb for artifact size without improving the primary metric.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: This isolates the deployment-path question the pr1394 record already highlights, instead of burying export choice inside full retraining changes.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Strong artifact-selection family with same-checkpoint controls and a clean observable; honest because it stays inside the deployment lane.

hypotheses.name: Bounded Online Controller Family
hypotheses.mechanism: Replace step-only phase changes with a bounded controller that reads cheap online signals such as loss slope, update norm, or step time and emits discrete mode switches like recurrence entry, optimizer stance, or finisher entry.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: Global step is sufficient as the only phase variable on pr1394.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: The core claim is that the search object should be a closed-loop state-conditioned program; any thresholds only exist inside that new object and are not the object itself.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Test one minimal controller grammar with 2-3 signals and 3-4 discrete actions against the fixed step-based schedule, with matched controls and trigger-time diagnostics.
hypotheses.expected_observable: Controller runs should show repeatable trigger timing differences and a shifted downstream versus pre-quant tradeoff before final ranking.
hypotheses.falsifier: Trigger traces collapse to fixed-step behavior, or controller variants fail to separate from controls on downstream sliding-window bpb.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: This promotes the stranded stage3_2 plus stage3_3 controller idea into the actual unit of search instead of another patch slate.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Mechanistically strong and distinct, but blocked honestly because the active pr1394 harness does not yet expose a live bounded controller primitive.

hypotheses.name: Downstream-First Lane-Split Admission Reset
hypotheses.mechanism: Run this enigma cycle as a strict downstream-first admission process: compare families only on quantized sliding-window bpb when present, keep pre-quant bpb as diagnostic evidence, and require directional downstream support against matched controls before promotion.
hypotheses.broken_invariant: A mixed or proxy ranking is good enough even when the real objective is deployed sliding-window bpb.
hypotheses.not_local_tuning_reason: This changes the comparison contract for the whole cycle, including what survives and what gets retired; it is not a parameter change inside any family.
hypotheses.smallest_decisive_probe: Take 2 controls and up to 6 mixed-family candidates, score them with explicit downstream-first ranking plus separate pre-quant and control-spread readouts, and compare the survivor set against a blended-ranking baseline.
hypotheses.expected_observable: Some candidates that look acceptable on pre-quant bpb should be filtered once downstream sliding-window bpb is made primary, yielding cleaner family rankings and fewer false promotions.
hypotheses.falsifier: Downstream-first and blended admission choose the same survivors within noise, implying lane mixing is not the harness bottleneck here.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_history: This directly attacks the documented stage3 failure mode of single-metric lane mixing, so it tests the campaign harness itself rather than another training diff.
hypotheses.self_check_summary: Valid reset-level move with aligned measurement and no fake machinery; it is consequential because it changes the survival law for the whole track.

rejected_lines: Standalone retunes of MUON_MOMENTUM_WARMUP_STEPS, ENABLE_LOOPING_AT, or similar scalars are not families; they stay inside an unproven fixed schedule.
A pure SDClip constant sweep without an explicit selector or deployment-program change is just threshold tuning disguised as a deployment hypothesis.
Adding more checkpoint logging by itself is instrumentation, not a consequential search family, unless paired with an artifact-selection policy.
